description_long: This object is a large, rectangular solar energy infrastructure platform featuring multiple solar panel arrays mounted on a flat base. The panels are arranged in two main clusters, each consisting of several individual photovoltaic modules with a grid-like pattern. The base structure appears to be made of a sturdy material, possibly metal or reinforced composite, and includes several raised black components that may serve as electrical housings or mounting supports. There are also smaller protrusions and a possible antenna or sensor on one end, suggesting integrated monitoring or communication equipment. The surface of the panels is dark and reflective, while the base has a bluish-gray tone with some black and metallic accents. The overall design is industrial and robust, suitable for installation in urban environments such as rooftops, parking structures, or dedicated solar farms.
description: A large, rectangular solar platform with multiple grid-patterned solar panels mounted on a sturdy bluish-gray base, featuring black support structures and possible monitoring equipment.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the platform, with two main solar panel clusters and several black support structures visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the platform and the raised black components, with the grid pattern of the solar panels clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the opposite side of the platform, showing the arrangement of the solar panels and the base's angular edges.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the length of the platform and the distribution of the solar panels and support structures.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are flat or nearly flat, with a slight tilt under 10 degrees; orientation appears fixed.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a robust metal frame, likely aluminum or steel.
attribute_array_configuration: Two main clusters of solar panels arranged in parallel rows, each cluster containing multiple panels in a grid-like layout.
